REPLY DECLARATION OF MARIA A. CARUANA IN SUPPORT OF MOTION FOR TERMINATING SANCTIONS OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER DISCOVERY RESPONSES AND REQUEST FOR ADDITIONAL MONETARY SANCTIONS (TRANSACTION ID # 57032672) FILED BY DEFENDANT CASA THAI MARKET, INC. LI, HUNG QUOC

DISCOVERY 302, DEFENDANT CASA THAI MARKET, INC., HUNG LI'S MOTION FOR TERMINATING SANCTIONS AND REQUEST FOR ADDITIONAL MONETARY SANCTIONS OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER DISCOVERY RESPONSES AND REQUEST FOR ADDITIONAL MONETARY SANCTIONS IS GRANTED. NO OPPOSITION FILED. SAID FURTHER RESPONSES AND RELATED DOCUMENTS SHALL BE SERVED WITHIN TWENTY DAYS OF SERVICE OF THIS ORDER WITH NOTICE OF ENTRY. THIS IS THE THIRD ORDER OF THIS COURT DIRECTING THE PLAINTIFF TO RESPOND TO DISCOVERY. THE TWO PREVIOUS ORDERS ARE DATED DECEMBER 5, 2014 AND JANUARY 13, 2015 AND BOTH HAVE BEEN IGNORED. PLAINTIFF SHALL ALSO RESPOND WITHIN TWENTY DAYS TO THIS COURT'S TWO PAST DISCOVERY ORDERS OF THIS COURT. FAILURE TO TIMELY RESPOND TO ALL THE DISCOVERY ORDERED HEREIN SHALL RESULT A TERMINATING SANCTION ON FURTHER WRITTEN MOTION OF THE DEFENDANT. SANCTIONS IN THE SUM OF $655.00 ARE AWARDED TO THE MOVING PARTY TO BE PAID WITHIN TWENTY DAYS OF THE DATE OF SERVICE OF THIS ORDER. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE PRO TEM: STEVEN STEIN. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. COURT REPORTER: KIMBERLY HENDERSHOTT, CSR# 12552, 877-451-1580. REPORTED. (302/JPT)

OPPOSITION TO DEMURRER FILED BY PLAINTIFF PAZOS, HECTOR

LAW AND MOTION, 302, NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. RULING: THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANTS CASA THAI MARKET, INC. AND HUNG LI'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED. ALLEGATIONS THAT AN EVENT OCCURRED "ON OR ABOUT" THE CRUCIAL DATE FOR STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS PURPOSES OVERCOME A GENERAL DEMURRER. IT IS ENOUGH THAT THE CLAIM MAY BE TIMELY. (CHILDS V. STATE OF CALIFORNIA (1983) 144 CA3D 155, 160.) (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, NOT REPORTED.

DEFENDANT CASA THAI MARKET, INC., HUNG LI'S Motion For Terminating Sanctions And Request For Additional Monetary Sanctions Or In The Alternative, Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses And Request For Additional Monetary Sanctions is granted. No opposition filed. Said further responses and related documents shall be served within twenty days of service of this Order with Notice of Entry. This is the third Order of this Court directing the plaintiff to respond to discovery. The two previous Orders are dated December 5, 2014 and January 13, 2015 and both have been ignored. Plaintiff shall also respond within twenty days to this court's two past discovery Orders of this Court. Failure to timely respond to all the discovery ordered herein shall result a terminating sanction on further written motion of the defendant. Sanctions in the sum of $655.00 are awarded to the moving party to be paid within twenty days of the date of service of this Order. Order signed.

Defendants Casa Thai Market, Inc. And Hung Li'S Motion Compel Further Discovery Responses And Request For Monetary Sanctions Or In The Alternative Request For Terminating Sanctions (For Entry Of Tentative Ruling Only)
Off Calendar Jan-13-2015 Text Ruling

Notice Of Motion And Motion Compel Further Discovery Responses And Request For Monetary Sanctions Or In The Alternative Request For Terminating Sanctions
Off Calendar Jan-13-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Tina W. Lu, Esq. of the Law Offices of John A. Biard, Tel. 925.945.4003, for defendant and moving party;
No other appearance.

RULING: The tentative ruling was not contested and adopted as the final ruling of the Court. DEFENDANTs CASA THAI MARKET, INC. and HUNG LI's Motion Compel Further Discovery Responses And Request For Monetary Sanctions Or In The Alternative Request For Terminating Sanctions is unopposed and is granted as follows:
The court finds that Plaintiff failed to make a good faith effort to respond to Form Interrogatories .1, 9.2, 12.1, 12.2, 12.3, 12.4, 12.5, 12.7, 14.1 and 14.2 and as well as comply with the Court Order of October 24, 2014. The code requires that a party must make a reasonable and good faith effort to obtain the information. Regency Health Services, Inc. v. Superior Court (1998) 64 CA4th 1496"A party cannot plead ignorance to information which can be obtained from sources under his control." Deyo v. Kilbourne (1978) 84 CA3d 771,782. This includes a party's lawyer Smith v. Sup. Ct (Alfred) (1961) 189 CA 2d 6, agents or employees Gordon v. Sup. Ct. (U.Z.MFG.Co) (1984) 161 CA 3d 15,167-168, family members Jones v. Superior Court (Benny) (1981) 119 CA 3d 534, 552 and experts who have been retained by a party and designated as a trial witness. Sigerseth v. Superior Court(1972) 23 CA 3d 427,433. See Weil and Brown, Cal Prac. Guide: Civil Procedure Before Trial (TRG 2009) 8:1051-1060 This means that you can't just pawn off the responses to your client or spend an hour and dictate off the top of your head and then answer "inability to respond." See Sinaiko Healthcare Consulting, Inc. v. Pacific Healthcare Consultants (2007) 148 CA4th 390 The statute and the case law make it very clear that a party and the attorney must be proactive in obtaining the information to respond to the interrogatories. Plaintiff to serve further verified responses to the interrogatories no later than January 26, 2015.
The court finds that plaintiff failed to serve documents in response to Requests #1, 2, 5, 7, 8 and 9. Plaintiff to serve verified responses and produce documents no later than January 26, 2015.
Defendants' request for sanctions is granted. Plaintiff and his attorney of record to pay Defendants $485.00 no later than January 26, 2015. ORDER SIGNED. (Counsel walked away without filing the order with the clerk.) Judge Pro Tem: Katherine Gallo; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Tara Williams CSR # 13428, Tel. 510.451.1580

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-10-2014 Overruled
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS CASA THAI MARKET, INC. AND HUNG LI'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is overruled. Allegations that an event occurred "on or about" the crucial date for statute of limitations purposes overcome a general demurrer. It is enough that the claim may be timely. (Childs v. State of California (1983) 144 CA3d 155, 160.) (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Not reported.
